notes: Two genuine improvement loops completed. Loop 1 established a universal, non-phonetic mechanism (mishearing a stated feeling as a name, replying with the local first-introduction greeting) anchored on 'hungry' and self-identified a cross-language grammar mismatch (avoir/tener vs. to-be constructions in French/Spanish, plus a clunky Japanese clause). Loop 2 consumed that feedback, re-anchored the joke on 'tired' (to-be + adjective in all of English/French/Spanish, single natural predicate in Japanese), and raised provisional self-scores across the board while keeping the mechanism, brevity, and cultural portability intact. All scores are generator provisional self-scores only; no independent panel scoring was claimed.
